name: Busan
description: Korea's most underrated gem — a gritty port city with stunning beaches, neon-lit fish markets, colorful hillside villages, and a raw, unpretentious energy that Seoul simply can't replicate.

quickInfo.visa: Visa-free 90 days for US/EU/AU. K-ETA required for many nationalities

hotels.name: YHA Korea Busan Hostel
hotels.description: Clean, well-run hostel near BIFF Square and Jagalchi Fish Market, perfect for solo travelers

hotels.name: Lotte Hotel Busan
hotels.description: Central luxury hotel with Lotte Department Store access and excellent breakfast buffet

hotels.name: Novotel Haeundae Busan
hotels.description: Beachfront hotel directly on Haeundae, Korea's most famous urban beach

hotels.name: Park Hyatt Busan
hotels.description: Glass skyscraper hotel facing the sea with breathtaking beach and skyline panoramas

hotels.name: Westin Josun Beach Hotel Busan
hotels.description: Historic luxury beachfront hotel, a Busan landmark since 1978, impeccably renovated

activities.name: Jagalchi Fish Market & Seafood Breakfast Tour
activities.description: Explore Korea's largest fish market at dawn and eat the freshest sashimi in Asia

activities.name: Gamcheon Culture Village Walking Tour
activities.description: Explore the 'Machu Picchu of Busan' — a maze of colorful hillside murals and art installations

activities.name: Haedong Yonggungsa Temple & Coast Walk
activities.description: Visit Korea's most dramatically situated seaside Buddhist temple with coastal path walk

activities.name: Busan City Highlights & Beach Day Tour
activities.description: Haeundae Beach, BIFF Square, Nampo-dong, Songdo Beach and cable car — comprehensive city tour

activities.name: Songdo Beach Zip Line & Ocean Walk
activities.description: Korea's longest ocean zip line (470m) over Songdo Beach cove, plus cliff walkway

items.activity: Gwangalli Beach walk & Gwangan Bridge views
Haeundae Makgeolli alley — Korean street food dinner
Dalmaji Hill hilltop art village & sea view cafes
Marine City illuminated skyline at dusk
Gwangalli Beach: ramyeon tents & sea view beer
Songdo Beach zip line & ocean cliff walk
Centum City Shinsegae (world's largest dept store)
Farewell dinner: dwaeji-gukbap (pork soup) in Seomyeon

practicalInfo.gettingThere: Gimhae International Airport (PUS) has direct flights from Tokyo, Osaka, Shanghai, Taipei, and other Asian cities. From Seoul: KTX bullet train 2h15min (~$45), or fly 50min.
practicalInfo.localTransport: Busan Metro covers all major areas. T-Money card works on metro and buses. Taxis cheap and plentiful. Walking between Haeundae, Gwangalli, Centum City is easy.
